pattern: A subarray is complete once it has grown to hold every distinct value in the whole array, and growing it further (in either direction) can never lose that property -- which turns the search into a sliding window that finds, for each right end, the smallest left end still complete, then counts every smaller left end for free.
hints: First figure out how many distinct values the whole array has -- that is the fixed target every complete subarray must reach.

For a fixed right end, once a window [left, right] contains all of those distinct values, shrinking further from the left may still leave it complete, and every valid left position also stays valid for any larger right end -- so a two-pointer sweep works.

Expand right one step at a time, updating a running frequency map; whenever the map's size equals the whole array's distinct count, shrink from the left while it stays complete, counting one complete subarray for every valid left position at this right end.

edge_inputs.description: every element distinct, only the whole array is complete
a single-element array is trivially complete
two distinct elements at minimum, only the full pair qualifies
two equal elements, every subarray is complete
paired duplicates for each distinct value
a long run of one value followed by a single rare value
a single value repeated at the top of the value range

stress.description: random array with a small value alphabet to create dense duplicates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
